prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for benzaldehyde (14 atoms) using the fukui-reactivity skill at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard', exact 4-centre integrals — no density fitting), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used doublet multiplicity (cation +1, anion −1). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). Three separate DFT SCF runs were performed (neutral, cation, anion), all converged cleanly; integrity.trustworthy = true, no warnings reported.

Headline result: the most electrophilic atom (highest f⁺, site of nucleophilic attack) is Atom 3 (carbonyl carbon C), with f⁺ = 0.39938965. The most nucleophilic atom (highest f⁻, site of electrophilic attack) is Atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen O), with f⁻ = 0.23839906. These results are fully consistent with the known chemistry of benzaldehyde: the carbonyl carbon is electrophilic and susceptible to nucleophilic addition, while the oxygen is the Lewis-basic/nucleophilic center. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000.

Command: cli.py fukui benzaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output: benzaldehyde_fukui_dft.json; bar chart plot also generated.
